hero.heading: @love-rox/tcy-react
hero.tagline: A `<Tcy>` component for React 17+. Walks children recursively and wraps half-width alphanumeric runs in `<span class="tcy">`. Produces deterministic, SSR-safe output.

install.heading: Install
install.description: `@love-rox/tcy-core` comes along as a dependency — no separate install needed.

usage.heading: Usage
usage.description: Wrap the vertical content in `<Tcy>` inside an element with `writing-mode: vertical-rl`. Only string children are rewritten; element children are walked recursively without being touched.

options.heading: Options
options.description: `<Tcy>` accepts the options below. The shared options behave identically to the Vue, rehype, and Astro adapters.

behavior.heading: Behavior notes
items: Runs are not joined across element boundaries — `1234` produces two separate spans.
Full-width alphanumerics (`Ａ–Ｚ` / `０–９`) are left alone because they already render upright in vertical text.
SSR-safe — server and client emit the same DOM, so there's no hydration mismatch.
Children that contain React elements are still walked recursively; text inside those elements is processed too.

related.heading: Other adapters
related.description: Using Vue? The Vue adapter has the same API. On a Markdown / Astro pipeline? The rehype and Astro adapters do this at build time, with no React dependency in the bundle.
